pattern: Unfolding the reflections turns the bouncing ray into a straight line, so the answer is decided by the parities of how many room-widths and room-heights it crosses before hitting a corner.
hints: Instead of tracking bounces, imagine reflecting the room repeatedly so the ray travels in a straight line and ask which stacked corner it reaches.
The ray first hits a corner after travelling a whole number of room widths horizontally and the same total height vertically, which is the least common multiple of p and q.
Reduce p and q by their gcd; if the reduced p is even the ray lands at receptor 2, otherwise an even reduced q gives receptor 0 and an odd reduced q gives receptor 1.

edge_inputs.description: ray reflects once and reaches the far-left top corner
odd over odd lands at receptor 1
q equals p, immediate diagonal to the top-right corner
common factor reduces to an even width
common factor reduces to odd over odd
even reduced width regardless of larger numbers
odd reduced parameters with a gcd above one

stress.description: random p with a random q not exceeding it, covering all parity combinations
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    p = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    q = rng.randint(1, p)
    return [p, q]
